limitations: no real participants — every answer is role-played from the persona + brief, so no new fact is discovered
insights are hypotheses to validate with real operators, not findings

questions.text: Walk me through the last time a guest arrived at your desk — what were you trying to get done?
When someone shows up without a booking reference, what do you actually do first?
Tell me about a check-in that went badly recently — what happened?
What slows the queue down the most on a busy morning?
Which parts of the current system do you avoid or work around?
At the end of a shift, what makes you feel it went well?
When an operator escalates a blocked action to you, what do you need to see before you decide?
Describe a time you couldn't tell why an override had happened — what did that cost you?
What does a good day look like from the back office?
How do you track what the front desk changed during the day?
What would let you trust the desk to handle exceptions without you?
What information do you wish you had that you don't today?

simulated_responses.answer: I ask for their name and try to find them that way, but the system only really searches by the booking code, so I end up asking them to dig through their email for it.
simulated_responses.quote: The system only searches by booking code, so I make the guest find the email.

simulated_responses.answer: Honestly it's the people without a reference. One of those and the whole line stops while they scroll their phone.
simulated_responses.quote: One guest without a reference and the whole line stops.

simulated_responses.answer: An operator overrode a blocked rate and I only found out from the nightly report — there was no note on why, so I couldn't back them up.
simulated_responses.quote: There was no note on why the override happened, so I couldn't back them up.

affinity_map.insight: Reference-only search offloads the work onto the guest and stalls the queue — operators want to find people by any attribute.
affinity_map.research_question: On arrival, do operators actually reach for name search before reference search?

affinity_map.insight: Overrides without a captured reason erode manager trust and can't be defended after the fact.
affinity_map.research_question: Do managers need a reason captured at override time, or is a nightly summary enough?

gate_log.reason: first answers paraphrased the persona's frustration verbatim (circular) — rewrote Q02/Q04 to ask for a concrete recent incident
answers now cite specific behaviour + trace to PP01/JTBD; two distinct quotes support each theme
